justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct classifications. Refund window (BR-7) correctly marked invariant: false with sound reasoning. Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly captured in BR-1. Discount code and promotion mutual exclusivity captured in BR-4. Multiple ambiguities surfaced (discount code behavior on mixed promoted/non-promoted orders, price-lock during checkout, zero-inventory check timing, 30-day boundary semantics). 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 82 is appropriate with clearly stated assumptions and low-confidence areas. Minor note: BR-2 (15-minute timeout) was classified as invariant: false, which is a defensible interpretation (the duration is policy, the mechanism is invariant) though the rubric expected invariant: true. The reasoning is sound and explicitly distinguishes the principle from the parameter, so this does not warrant a downgrade.

observations.text: All 7 business rules identified and well-articulated with strong justifications
BR-7 refund window correctly classified as invariant: false with clear policy vs integrity reasoning
Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction is prominent — BR-1 explicitly states cart does NOT reserve inventory
Discount code and promotion mutual exclusivity clearly captured in BR-4 with revenue leakage justification
12 acceptance criteria with excellent coverage including concurrency scenarios (AC-11, AC-12)
Multiple ambiguities surfaced: mixed promoted/non-promoted discount application, price-lock during checkout, zero-inventory check timing
Rich edge cases section covering race conditions, boundary days, mid-checkout price changes, and disputed deliveries
Comprehensive business impact analysis with specific stakeholder identification

BR-2 reservation timeout classified as invariant: false — defensible but differs from expected invariant: true. The split reasoning (principle vs duration) is novel but could cause downstream confusion if consumed literally
Did not flag the specific ambiguity about whether the 15-minute timer starts at checkout page load or first item addition, but surfaced other meaningful ambiguities that meet the rubric threshold
